AppendToFileAction.description: Append content to an existing file (or create if absent).
AppendToFileAction.title: AppendToFileAction

AppendToFileFix.description: Append content to an existing file (or create if absent).
AppendToFileFix.title: AppendToFileFix

CompositeMatch.description:
Boolean composition over OSS leaf match clauses.

Each leaf clause is "satisfied" when its matcher returns no
findings (the required paths exist, the required regex matches,
the manifest field is set, …). Composite ops are defined over
that per-clause satisfied / unsatisfied bit:

- ``and``: composite is satisfied iff every clause is satisfied.
  When any clause is unsatisfied, emits one composite finding
  summarising which conjunct(s) failed (callers typically use
  ``summary`` to carry the human-readable conjunction text).
- ``or``: composite is satisfied iff at least one clause is
  satisfied. When ALL clauses are unsatisfied, emits one finding
  per failing clause so the user sees each unmet alternative,
  prefixed by ``summary`` (if set) so the reporter can label the
  all-fail case explicitly.
- ``not``: composite is satisfied iff the (single) clause is
  unsatisfied. Used to express "X is missing"; emits one finding
  naming what ``X`` was when the inner clause was satisfied.

Note on ``or``: an earlier revision of this docstring described
``or`` as "fires when any clause produces ≥1 finding" (i.e.
OR-of-failures). That wording was inverted relative to how every
rule in the agent-readiness rules pack uses ``or``  — "any of
these alternatives is acceptable" — and was producing 6+
spurious findings per scanned repo. The semantic above
(OR-of-satisfactions) is the corrected, canonical contract.

Nested ``CompositeMatch`` clauses are allowed up to a small depth
(engines should cap recursion at 4 to keep evaluation predictable).
``PrivateMatch`` clauses are also accepted so composite expressions
can incorporate downstream-engine analyses.

summary.description: Optional human-readable summary used as the composite finding message.
summary.title: Summary

CompositeMatch.title: CompositeMatch

ContextProbe.description:
A single probe declared on an action.

``detect`` names the signal to capture from the repo; the engine
binds the result to ``{<detect>}`` placeholders inside
``action.template``. Probes that the engine cannot resolve emit
an empty string so templates degrade gracefully.
ContextProbe.title: ContextProbe

ContextProbeKind.description:
Probes the engine runs at scan time to populate ``{variable}``s
inside an ``action.template``. Each kind is deterministic — no LLM
in the action path.
ContextProbeKind.title: ContextProbeKind

CreateFileAction.description: Materialise a brand-new file with the given content / template.

path.description: Repo-relative path of the new file.
path.title: Path

template.description: Full file contents to create. May contain ``{variable}`` placeholders populated by ``context_probe`` at scan time.
template.title: Template

CreateFileAction.title: CreateFileAction

CreateFileFix.description:
Materialise a brand-new file with the given contents.

Diffed against an empty buffer so dashboards render the whole file
as an added hunk.

path.description: Repo-relative path of the new file.
path.title: Path

CreateFileFix.title: CreateFileFix

EditGitignoreAction.description: Append entries to ``.gitignore`` (creating it if absent).

entries.description: Glob patterns to append, one per line.
entries.title: Entries

EditGitignoreAction.title: EditGitignoreAction

InsertAfterAction.description: Insert content immediately after the first line matching ``after_pattern``.

after_pattern.description: Python re-style regex matched against the file with re.MULTILINE.
after_pattern.title: After Pattern

InsertAfterAction.title: InsertAfterAction

InsertAfterFix.description: Insert content immediately after the first line matching ``after_pattern``.

after_pattern.description: Python re-style regex matched against the file with re.MULTILINE.
after_pattern.title: After Pattern

InsertAfterFix.title: InsertAfterFix

field_path.description: Dotted path to a required field, e.g. 'project.scripts'.
field_path.title: Field Path

ModifyManifestFieldAction.description:
Set / merge a field inside a structured manifest (TOML/JSON/YAML).

``value`` is a string the engine inserts under ``field_path``. For
JSON / YAML the engine parses-edit-rewrites; for TOML it appends a
fresh table if the field is missing.

field_path.description: Dotted path to the target field.
field_path.title: Field Path

ModifyManifestFieldAction.title: ModifyManifestFieldAction

Pillar.description: Mirrors agent_readiness.models.Pillar so the schema stays in sync.
Pillar.title: Pillar

Precondition.description:
A simple structural precondition the engine evaluates before
emitting the action.

All non-null fields must hold for the action to fire. Omit fields
that are not relevant. Used by ``rules_pack >= 2`` to keep actions
targeted (e.g. don't suggest editing a Makefile when none exists).

exists.description: Repo-relative path that must exist for this action to fire.
exists.title: Exists

not_exists.description: Repo-relative path that must NOT exist for this action to fire.
not_exists.title: Not Exists

Precondition.title: Precondition

PrivateMatch.description:
Catch-all for non-OSS (private) match types.

Downstream engines (e.g. ``agent-readiness-pro`` or the closed
insights engine) extend the rules pack with bespoke analyses such as
``git_log_query``, ``ast_complexity``, or ``regex_secret_scan``.
These are registered in the engine via
``register_private_matcher(type_name, fn)`` and are referenced from
YAML rules as ``match.type: <name>``.

The protocol does not specify the *shape* of a PrivateMatch — every
field beyond ``type`` is engine-specific — so this model accepts
arbitrary extra fields. ``type`` is constrained only to forbid the
six OSS match-type names, preventing a malformed OSS rule from
silently being parsed as a private one. Engines that do not know
a given private type produce a ``not_measured`` finding rather
than crashing.

type.description: Engine-specific match type name; must NOT collide with an OSS type.
type.title: Type

PrivateMatch.title: PrivateMatch

RunCommandAction.description:
Run a shell command in the repo root.

Used for actions that aren't a single file edit (initialising a
devcontainer, scaffolding a CI workflow with `gh extension`, etc.).
The command MUST be deterministic and offline-safe; agents may
refuse to execute network calls.

command.description: Shell command to run from the repo root.
command.title: Command

RunCommandAction.title: RunCommandAction

VerifyStep.description:
The verification command an agent runs after applying an action.

Must exit 0 when the rule would no longer fire on the modified
repo. Should be deterministic, offline (network is forbidden), and
fast (≤ ``timeout_seconds``).

command.description: Shell command; exit 0 = fix landed, non-zero = still missing.
command.title: Command

VerifyStep.title: VerifyStep

description:
Declarative rule. Loaded from YAML; produced as JSON by `/rules`.

`rules_version` is the schema version of THIS rule file; loaders
refuse rules whose rules_version is outside their supported range.

For ``rules_version >= 2`` the rule MUST carry an ``action`` block
and a ``verify`` step (the deterministic action contract). Older
v1 rules are still accepted during the transition window; their
legacy ``fix_template`` field is automatically mapped to ``action``
by the engine when emitting findings.

action.description: Structured deterministic action — the agent's instruction for what to change, where, and how. Required for ``rules_version >= 2``. Carries optional ``preconditions`` and ``context_probe`` blocks so the engine can gate emission and substitute repo signals into ``{variable}`` placeholders.
action.title: Action

explanation.description: Why this rule predicts an agent failure mode.
explanation.title: Explanation

fix_template.description: Deprecated as of rules_version 2 — use ``action`` instead. Retained for backward compatibility; engines auto-map it onto the matching ``Action`` model when surfacing findings.
fix_template.title: Fix Template

insight_query.description: Free-text query the engine uses to retrieve related insights for findings of this rule.
insight_query.title: Insight Query

provenance.description: Attribution string identifying the upstream origin of this rule. Required so every rule that ships with this protocol carries a clear authorship trail. Format: ``agent-readiness/<rule-id>`` for native rules, ``<owner>/<repo>#<anchor>`` for ports of other open-source rule packs (e.g. ``microsoft/agentrc#docs.agents-md``). The field is mandatory; contributors who fork this protocol must preserve the provenance line of any rule they redistribute.
provenance.title: Provenance

rules_version.description: Schema version this rule conforms to. Defaults to the lowest supported version so legacy YAMLs without an explicit ``rules_version:`` line still validate. New rules should set this to RULES_VERSION (the latest) and supply an ``action`` + ``verify`` pair. Loaders gate on this.
rules_version.title: Rules Version

verify.description: Verification step the agent runs after applying ``action`` to confirm the fix landed. Required for ``rules_version >= 2``.

title: Rule
